title: Environment, Shell State, Startup Files, and Sessions · Terminal Trace: Shell Variables Environment
summary: Repair a command that works interactively but fails in automation by tracing environment and startup differences instead of adding global hacks. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux shell variables environment increment in an environment-diff and startup-resolution notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.
Interpret nice values, scheduling priority, resource limits, and pressure evidence without equating priority with guaranteed resources.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.
